title: Termination, Budgets, Timeouts, and Progress Detection · Spiral Review
summary: Stop an agent safely under success, failure, budget, and no-progress conditions. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a loop termination and budget policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Terminate only when completion evidence satisfies the goal contract.
Classify retryable, recoverable, authority-blocked, and terminal failures.
Budget turns, tokens, time, tool calls, money, and external side effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.title: Success Stop · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: loops termination budgets. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Terminate only when completion evidence satisfies the goal contract. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: loops termination budgets. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Success Stop, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a loop termination and budget policy.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A fluent final message is a success condition. This fails the Success Stop contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

Use a changed review case derived from this module: loops termination budgets. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

stimulus.title: Graceful Handoff case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: loops termination budgets. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: When a budget ends, incomplete work should be called complete.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Stop with a resumable state, evidence, and explicit unmet conditions. Artifact under review: a loop termination and budget policy.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Graceful Handoff, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Graceful Handoff applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Stop with a resumable state, evidence, and explicit unmet conditions.
